# httpwire seed corpus

Raw HTTP/1.1 response bytes, captured verbatim off the wire from the real
CSIP grid simulator (`csip-tls-test/sim/gridsim`), not synthesized. Each
file is exactly what `internal/tlsclient.(*Client).readResponse` (now
`httpwire.ReadHTTPResponse`) would have read from the wolfSSL session for
that request ‚Äî status line through the last body byte, `Connection: close`
so the capture script could read to EOF without racing a keep-alive server.

TLS is irrelevant to this corpus: `ReadHTTPResponse` only ever sees
already-decrypted bytes, so a plain-TCP capture against gridsim's
`httptest.Server` (no wolfSSL/cgo needed to generate these) has identical
wire-format provenance to a capture taken through the real mTLS path.
Self-confirmation caveat (review ¬ß9): the corpus is generated by the same
server family this parser is written against, so it exercises "gridsim says
this" inputs well but says nothing about servers that violate HTTP more
creatively ‚Äî that's what the fuzzer explores from here.

## Capture method

From a checkout of `csip-tls-test` (module resolves `lexa-proto` via
go.work or the vendored fallback ‚Äî see that repo's `go.work` /
`AD-003(e)` vendoring note):

```go
// tests/zzz_capture_corpus_test.go (temporary; not committed)
sim := gridsim.NewServer(testLFDI)
ts := httptest.NewServer(sim.Handler())
addr := ts.Listener.Addr().String()
conn, _ := net.Dial("tcp", addr)
conn.Write([]byte("GET /dcap HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: " + addr +
	"\r\nAccept: application/sep+xml\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n"))
raw, _ := io.ReadAll(conn) // gridsim closes; raw is the exact wire bytes
```

Run: `GOWORK=off CAPTURE_CORPUS_DIR=/some/dir go test ./tests/ -run TestCaptureHTTPWireCorpus -v`
(POST bodies use the analogous `rawPost` helper with a real
`Content-Length`.) Then copy the `*.raw` files here and delete the temporary
test file ‚Äî it is scaffolding for corpus generation, not a permanent test.

## Files

| File | Request | What it exercises |
|---|---|---|
| `dcap.raw` | `GET /dcap` | DeviceCapability ‚Äî the canonical happy path (Content-Length, `application/sep+xml`) |
| `time.raw` | `GET /tm` | small resource, exact Content-Length match |
| `edev-list.raw` | `GET /edev` | EndDeviceList, larger body |
| `der-program-list.raw` | `GET /edev/2/fsa/0/derp` | DERProgramList ‚Äî nested links |
| `der-control-list.raw` | `GET /derp/0/derc` | DERControlList ‚Äî largest capture, several list entries |
| `default-der-control.raw` | `GET /derp/0/dderc` | DefaultDERControl |
| `mup-list.raw` | `GET /mup` | MirrorUsagePointList |
| `response-set-list.raw` | `GET /rsps` | ResponseSetList |
| `404.raw` | `GET /this-resource-does-not-exist` | error status, zero-length body, still `Content-Length: 0` framed |
| `post-mup-201.raw` | `POST /mup` | 201 Created with `Location` header, zero-length body |
| `post-response-201.raw` | `POST /rsps/0/r` | POST reply (RSP.00x acknowledgement path), zero-length body |
| `redirect-301.raw` | `GET /dcap-old` | 301 + `Location` header (WP-3 redirect following, ERR-001) |
| `redirect-302.raw` | `GET /dcap-old` | 302 + `Location` header ‚Äî the CSIP rediscovery shape (WP-3/D3) |

All thirteen are real `net/http`-server-generated bytes (Go's `net/http`
server, same as gridsim runs behind wolfSSL in production) ‚Äî none are
hand-written. The two `redirect-*.raw` captures (WP-3) come from a stock
`net/http` `http.Redirect` handler behind `httptest` rather than gridsim
itself ‚Äî gridsim emits no 30x today ‚Äî captured with the exact raw-socket
method above, so their wire-format provenance matches the rest.
